oxygen.en: a gas in the air that people and animals need to breathe

ex.en: Plants produce oxygen during photosynthesis.

pace.en: walk steadily, especially back and forth, often because you are nervous or thinking hard

ex.en: He paced up and down the hallway, waiting for news.
ex.ko: 그는 소식을 기다리며 복도를 왔다 갔다 걸었다.

pace.en: the speed at which someone walks, runs, or moves

ex.en: She walked at a slow pace along the beach.

pace.en: the speed at which something happens or changes

ex.en: The pace of change in technology is incredible.

package.en: a box or container in which something is wrapped or sent

ex.en: A package arrived for you this morning.
ex.ko: 오늘 아침에 당신 앞으로 소포가 도착했어요.

package.en: a set of things offered or sold together as a single unit

ex.en: The hotel offers a package that includes breakfast and a tour.
ex.ko: 그 호텔은 조식과 투어가 포함된 패키지를 제공한다.

packing.en: the activity of putting things into bags or boxes, especially for a trip

ex.en: I still have to finish my packing before the trip.
ex.ko: 여행 전에 짐 싸기를 아직 끝내야 한다.

paddle.en: a short pole with a wide flat end used to move a small boat through water

ex.en: He gripped the paddle tightly and pushed off from the shore.
ex.ko: 그는 노를 꽉 잡고 해안에서 배를 밀어냈다.

paddle.en: move a small boat through water using a paddle

ex.en: They paddled the canoe across the lake.

paddle.en: play or walk about in shallow water

ex.en: The children paddled happily in the shallow water.

paid.en: receiving or providing money for work done

ex.en: She gets two weeks of paid vacation every year.

pain.en: the physical feeling of being hurt or sick

ex.en: She felt a sharp pain in her back.

pain.en: someone or something that is very annoying (informal)

ex.en: This broken printer is such a pain.
Losing the game was a painful experience for the team.
ex.ko: 그 경기에서 진 것은 팀에게 고통스러운 경험이었다.

pale.en: having skin that is lighter in color than usual, often because of illness or fear

ex.en: She looked pale after hearing the bad news.
ex.ko: 그녀는 나쁜 소식을 듣고 창백해 보였다.

pale.en: (of a color) light rather than dark or bright

ex.en: The walls were painted a pale blue.

pale.en: become pale, especially from fear or shock

ex.en: He paled at the sight of the accident.

palm.en: the inner, flat surface of your hand

ex.en: She held the coin in the palm of her hand.

palm.en: a tall tropical tree with large leaves at the top and no branches

ex.en: Palm trees lined the beach.

parachute.en: a piece of equipment that opens up like a large umbrella to let a person or object fall slowly and safely from an aircraft

ex.en: He jumped out of the plane and opened his parachute.

paradise.en: a place or state of perfect happiness and beauty

ex.en: This tropical island feels like paradise.
ex.ko: 이 열대 섬은 마치 낙원 같다.

paralyze.en: make a person or part of the body unable to move or feel anything

ex.en: The accident left him paralyzed from the waist down.
ex.ko: 그 사고로 그는 허리 아래가 마비되었다.

paralyze.en: make a system, organization, or activity completely unable to function

ex.en: The heavy snow paralyzed the entire city.

parcel.en: something wrapped in paper or a box, especially to be sent by mail

ex.en: The postman delivered a parcel to our house.
Children need parental guidance and support.

parking.en: a space or area where vehicles can be left

ex.en: There is no free parking near the station.
ex.ko: 역 근처에는 무료 주차 공간이 없다.

parking.en: the act of leaving a vehicle somewhere for a period of time

ex.en: Parking here is only allowed for one hour.

parrot.en: a brightly colored tropical bird that can be taught to copy human speech

ex.en: The parrot repeated everything it heard.
ex.ko: 그 앵무새는 들은 것을 모두 따라 했다.

parrot.en: repeat someone else's words without really understanding them

ex.en: He just parrots whatever his boss says.
ex.ko: 그는 상사가 하는 말을 그대로 따라 할 뿐이다.

part-time.en: working or done for less than the usual full number of hours

ex.en: She has a part-time job at a cafe.

part-time.en: for less than the usual full number of hours

ex.en: He only works part-time on weekends.

partial.en: affecting or including only a part, not the whole

ex.en: The project was only a partial success.

partial.en: unfairly favoring one side over another

ex.en: The referee's decision seemed partial to the home team.
ex.ko: 그 심판의 판정은 홈팀에 편파적으로 보였다.

participant.en: a person who takes part in an activity or event

ex.en: Every participant received a certificate at the end of the workshop.
ex.ko: 모든 참가자는 워크숍이 끝날 때 수료증을 받았다.

participate.en: take part in an activity or event

ex.en: All students are encouraged to participate in the discussion.

participle.en: a form of a verb, ending in -ing or -ed, that can be used as an adjective or to make certain tenses

ex.en: "Broken" is the past participle of the verb "break".

particularly.en: more than usual or more than other things; especially

ex.en: I particularly enjoy reading mystery novels.
ex.ko: 나는 특히 추리소설 읽는 것을 좋아한다.

passion.en: a very strong feeling of enthusiasm or love for something

ex.en: She has a real passion for painting.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그림에 진정한 열정을 가지고 있다.

passive.en: accepting what happens without taking action or resisting

ex.en: He took a passive role in the negotiations.

passive.en: the grammatical form used when the subject receives the action of the verb

ex.en: "The window was broken" is written in the passive.
ex.ko: "The window was broken"은 수동태로 쓰인 문장이다.

passport.cue: 외국 여행 시 필요한 신분 증명서
passport.en: an official document that allows you to travel to other countries

ex.en: Don't forget to bring your passport to the airport.
ex.ko: 공항에 여권 챙겨 가는 것 잊지 마세요.

password.cue: 계정 로그인할 때 쓰는 비밀 단어
password.en: a secret word or set of characters used to log into an account

ex.en: Please choose a strong password for your account.

patience.en: the ability to stay calm and wait for a long time without complaining

ex.en: Teaching young children requires a lot of patience.
ex.ko: 어린아이들을 가르치는 데는 많은 인내심이 필요하다.

patrol.en: the activity of regularly walking or driving around an area to check that it is safe

ex.en: The guards are on patrol all night.

patrol.en: regularly walk or travel around an area to check that it is safe

ex.en: Police officers patrol the neighborhood every evening.
ex.ko: 경찰관들은 매일 저녁 그 동네를 순찰한다.

pattern.en: a repeated design of shapes, lines, or colors on something

ex.en: She chose a fabric with a floral pattern.

pattern.en: a regular way in which something happens or is done

ex.en: Researchers noticed a pattern in the data.

pause.en: a short period when an activity stops before continuing

ex.en: There was a long pause before she answered.
ex.ko: 그녀가 대답하기 전에 긴 정지가 있었다.

pause.en: stop doing something for a short time before continuing

ex.en: He paused to think before answering.
ex.ko: 그는 대답하기 전에 잠시 멈춰 생각했다.

peanut.en: a small nut that grows underground and is often eaten as a snack

ex.en: She is allergic to peanuts.
There was a peculiar smell in the kitchen.

peculiar.en: belonging only to a particular person, thing, or place

ex.en: This custom is peculiar to the region.
ex.ko: 이 관습은 이 지역에만 특유한 것이다.

ex.en: He arrived in the city penniless and alone.
ex.ko: 그는 무일푼으로 홀로 그 도시에 도착했다.

penny.en: a small coin worth very little, or a general word for a small amount of money

ex.en: I don't have a penny left in my pocket.
ex.ko: 내 주머니엔 동전 한 닢도 남아 있지 않다.

per.en: for each one of, used when giving a rate or amount

ex.en: The room costs 100 dollars per night.

percent.en: a way of expressing a number as a part of one hundred

ex.en: Fifty percent of the students passed the test.

performer.en: a person who performs in front of an audience, such as an actor, musician, or dancer

ex.en: The street performer drew a large crowd.
ex.ko: 그 거리 공연자는 많은 사람들을 끌어모았다.

permanent.en: lasting for a very long time or forever, without changing

ex.en: The injury caused permanent damage to his knee.
ex.ko: 그 부상은 그의 무릎에 영구적인 손상을 남겼다.

permanently.en: in a way that lasts forever or does not change

ex.en: The factory closed permanently last year.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 작년에 영구적으로 문을 닫았다.

permit.en: allow something to happen or allow someone to do something

ex.en: Smoking is not permitted inside the building.

permit.en: an official document that gives you permission to do something

ex.en: You need a permit to park here.

personally.en: used to say what you yourself think or feel about something

ex.en: Personally, I think the movie was too long.
ex.ko: 개인적으로 나는 그 영화가 너무 길었다고 생각한다.

persuade.en: make someone decide to do something by giving them good reasons

ex.en: She persuaded her parents to let her travel alone.
ex.ko: 그녀는 부모님을 설득해 혼자 여행하도록 허락받았다.

persuasive.en: able to make people believe or do something

ex.en: He gave a persuasive speech about climate change.
ex.ko: 그는 기후 변화에 대해 설득력 있는 연설을 했다.

phantom.en: a ghost, or something that seems to exist but is not real

ex.en: The old house is said to be haunted by a phantom.
ex.ko: 그 낡은 집에는 유령이 나온다고 전해진다.

pharmacy.en: a shop where medicines are sold

ex.en: I need to pick up my prescription at the pharmacy.

phenomenon.en: a fact or event, especially one that is unusual or interesting

ex.en: Global warming is a phenomenon that affects the whole planet.
ex.ko: 지구 온난화는 지구 전체에 영향을 미치는 현상이다.

philosopher.en: a person who studies or writes about deep questions of life and knowledge

ex.en: Socrates is one of the most famous Greek philosophers.
ex.ko: 소크라테스는 가장 유명한 그리스 철학자 중 한 명이다.

philosophy.en: the study of basic ideas about knowledge, truth, and existence

ex.en: She studied philosophy at university.

philosophy.en: a personal set of beliefs about how to live or deal with situations

ex.en: His philosophy is to always stay positive.
ex.ko: 그의 신조는 항상 긍정적으로 사는 것이다.

phoenix.en: a legendary bird that is said to burn up and then rise again from its ashes

ex.en: Like a phoenix, the city rose from the ashes of the war.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 불사조처럼 전쟁의 잿더미에서 다시 일어섰다.

photocopy.en: a copy of a document made with a photocopier

ex.en: Please make a photocopy of your passport.

photocopy.en: make a copy of a document using a photocopier

ex.en: Could you photocopy this page for me?

phrasal verb.en: a verb combined with a preposition or adverb that together have a special meaning

ex.en: "Give up" is a phrasal verb that means "to stop trying".

phrase.en: a group of words that together express an idea but is not a full sentence

ex.en: "In the morning" is a useful phrase to know.

physics.en: the scientific study of matter, energy, and how they interact

ex.en: He is majoring in physics at college.

pie.en: a food made of pastry filled with fruit, meat, or vegetables and baked

ex.en: She baked an apple pie for dessert.

piety.en: deep respect for God or religion and strong religious belief

ex.en: The old monk was known for his piety.

pillow.en: a soft cushion used to support your head while sleeping

ex.en: She fluffed up her pillow before going to sleep.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자기 전에 베개를 통통하게 두드렸다.

pin.en: a small, thin, pointed piece of metal used to fasten things together

ex.en: She used a pin to attach the name tag to her shirt.

pin.en: fasten something in place with a pin

ex.en: He pinned the notice to the board.

pin.en: a number you use to access an account, such as at an ATM

ex.en: Please enter your four-digit PIN.

pineapple.en: a large tropical fruit with a rough skin and sweet yellow flesh

ex.en: She cut the pineapple into small pieces.

pipe.en: a long tube made of metal or plastic used to carry water, oil, or gas

ex.en: Water flows through pipes under the street.
ex.ko: 물은 거리 아래의 파이프를 통해 흐른다.

pipe.en: a small object with a tube and bowl used for smoking tobacco

ex.en: His grandfather always smoked a pipe after dinner.
ex.ko: 그의 할아버지는 항상 저녁 식사 후 파이프 담배를 피웠다.

pit.en: a large hole in the ground

ex.en: The workers dug a deep pit to bury the pipes.
ex.ko: 인부들은 파이프를 묻기 위해 깊은 구덩이를 팠다.

pit.en: the hard seed found in the middle of some fruits, such as peaches or cherries

ex.en: Be careful not to bite the pit in the cherry.
ex.ko: 체리 안에 있는 씨를 깨물지 않게 조심해.

plain.en: simple in style, without decoration or extra features

ex.en: She wore a plain white T-shirt.

plain.en: easy to see, hear, or understand; clear

ex.en: It was plain that he was upset.

plain.cue: 나무나 산 없이 넓게 펼쳐진 땅
plain.en: a large area of flat land with few trees

ex.en: Herds of animals roam across the plain.

platform.en: the raised area at a train station where you get on or off a train

ex.en: The train will arrive at platform 3.

platform.en: the stated aims and principles of a political party

ex.en: The party's platform focuses on education reform.
ex.ko: 그 정당의 강령은 교육 개혁에 초점을 맞춘다.

platform.en: a type of computer system or an online service that other programs or activities are built on

ex.en: The app runs on both mobile and desktop platforms.
ex.ko: 그 앱은 모바일과 데스크톱 플랫폼 모두에서 실행된다.

pleasantly.en: in a way that is enjoyable or gives a good feeling

ex.en: I was pleasantly surprised by how good the food was.
ex.ko: 음식이 얼마나 맛있는지 기분 좋게 놀랐다.

plug.en: fill or close a hole tightly

ex.en: They plugged the leak with tape.

plug.en: a device with pins that connects an electrical appliance to a power socket

ex.en: Push the plug into the socket firmly.

plug.en: a small object used to fill and close a hole tightly, such as in a sink

ex.en: She pulled the plug and the water drained out.

pocket money.en: a small amount of money given regularly to a child, especially by parents

ex.en: She saves half of her pocket money every week.
She has admired that poet since high school.
ex.ko: 그녀는 고등학교 때부터 그 시인을 존경해 왔다.

poetry.en: writing that expresses ideas and feelings using rhythm and often rhyme

ex.en: He enjoys reading poetry before bed.
ex.ko: 그는 자기 전에 시를 읽는 것을 즐긴다.

poison.en: put poison into something, or harm someone or something using poison

ex.en: Someone poisoned the water supply.

poison.en: a substance that can harm or kill a living thing if it enters the body

ex.en: The snake's bite injects poison into its prey.
ex.ko: 그 뱀은 물면서 먹잇감에 독을 주입한다.

poisonous.en: containing poison, or able to cause injury or death if eaten or touched

ex.en: Some mushrooms in the forest are poisonous.

pole.en: a long, thin, straight piece of wood, metal, or plastic

ex.en: They used a long pole to reach the fruit on the tree.
ex.ko: 그들은 나무의 과일에 닿기 위해 긴 장대를 사용했다.

pole.en: either end of the earth's axis, or either end of a magnet

ex.en: Polar bears live near the North Pole.

policy.en: a plan of action agreed on by a government, company, or organization

ex.en: The company changed its refund policy.

policy.en: a written contract with an insurance company

ex.en: Check your policy to see what it covers.
ex.ko: 무엇이 보장되는지 보험 증권을 확인해 보세요.

politely.en: in a way that shows good manners and respect for others

ex.en: She politely asked the waiter for the bill.

politician.en: a person who works in politics, especially as an elected member of government

ex.en: Local politicians promised to fix the roads.

politics.en: the activities and ideas connected with governing a country

ex.en: She is not very interested in politics.

pollutant.en: a substance that pollutes the air, water, or soil

ex.en: Factories release harmful pollutants into the air.
ex.ko: 공장들은 해로운 오염물질을 대기 중으로 배출한다.

pond.en: a small area of water, smaller than a lake

ex.en: Ducks were swimming in the pond.

popcorn.cue: 영화 볼 때 먹는 튀긴 옥수수
popcorn.en: a snack made from corn kernels that are heated until they burst open

ex.en: We always buy popcorn before the movie starts.
ex.ko: 우리는 영화가 시작하기 전에 항상 팝콘을 산다.

ex.en: He grilled some pork for dinner.

port.en: a town or area by the sea or a river where ships load and unload goods or passengers

ex.en: The ship arrived at the port early in the morning.
ex.ko: 그 배는 이른 아침에 항구에 도착했다.

port.en: a place on a computer where you can connect a cable or device

ex.en: Plug the mouse into the USB port.

positive.en: hopeful and confident, or showing the good side of something

ex.en: She always tries to stay positive during hard times.
ex.ko: 그녀는 힘든 시기에도 항상 긍정적이려고 노력한다.

ex.en: Are you positive that you locked the door?

positive.en: showing that a particular substance or condition is present, as in a medical test

ex.en: His test result came back positive.

positively.en: in a way that shows hope or approval

ex.en: The teacher responded positively to the students' ideas.

positively.en: used to emphasize that something is completely true or certain

ex.en: The traffic was positively terrible this morning.
ex.ko: 오늘 아침 교통 체증은 정말로 끔찍했다.

ex.en: She possesses a rare talent for languages.
ex.ko: 그녀는 언어에 대한 드문 재능을 지니고 있다.

ex.en: He lost all his possessions in the fire.

possession.en: the state of having or owning something

ex.en: The document is now in his possession.
ex.ko: 그 문서는 지금 그가 소유하고 있다.

possessive.en: wanting to keep someone or something only for yourself

ex.en: He is very possessive about his tools.
ex.ko: 그는 자기 도구에 대해 소유욕이 매우 강하다.

possessive.en: (in grammar) showing that something belongs to someone

ex.en: "My" is the possessive form of "I".

possibility.en: the chance that something might happen or be true

ex.en: There's a possibility that the flight will be delayed.

postcard.en: a card, often with a picture, that you write a short message on and send by mail without an envelope

ex.en: She sent me a postcard from Paris.

postman.en: a person whose job is to deliver mail

ex.en: The postman delivers letters every morning at nine.

pot.en: a round metal or ceramic container used for cooking food

ex.en: She put the pot of soup on the stove.

pot.en: a container in which a plant is grown

ex.en: He planted the flower in a small pot.

potential.en: possible although not yet actual or developed

ex.en: The new drug shows potential benefits for patients.
ex.ko: 그 신약은 환자들에게 잠재적인 이점을 보인다.

potential.en: the ability or qualities someone has that could develop into success in the future

ex.en: The coach believes she has great potential.
ex.ko: 코치는 그녀가 뛰어난 잠재력을 가지고 있다고 믿는다.

pottery.en: objects such as pots and dishes made from clay that has been shaped and baked

ex.en: She took a class to learn how to make pottery.
ex.ko: 그녀는 도자기 만드는 법을 배우려고 수업을 들었다.

ex.en: He pounded on the door until someone answered.
ex.ko: 그는 누군가 대답할 때까지 문을 쿵쿵 두드렸다.

pound.en: a unit of weight equal to about 0.45 kilograms

ex.en: The baby weighed seven pounds at birth.

pound.en: the unit of money used in the United Kingdom

ex.en: The ticket costs twenty pounds.

poverty.en: the state of being very poor, having little or no money

ex.en: Millions of people around the world live in poverty.
ex.ko: 전 세계 수백만 명의 사람들이 빈곤 속에 살고 있다.

powder.en: break something into very small particles

ex.en: Powder the almonds before adding them to the batter.
ex.ko: 반죽에 넣기 전에 아몬드를 가루로 만들어라.

powder.en: a substance in the form of very small, loose particles

ex.en: Add a spoonful of curry powder to the sauce.
ex.ko: 소스에 카레 가루 한 숟가락을 넣어라.

practical.en: relating to real situations and actions rather than ideas or theories, and useful

ex.en: She gave me some practical advice about job hunting.
ex.ko: 그녀는 구직 활동에 대해 실용적인 조언을 해 주었다.

praise.en: express approval or admiration for someone or something

ex.en: The teacher praised the students for their hard work.

praise.en: words that show approval or admiration

ex.en: She received praise for her excellent performance.

prayer.en: words spoken to God, especially to give thanks or ask for help

ex.en: She said a prayer before going to bed.
ex.ko: 그녀는 잠자리에 들기 전에 기도를 했다.

precious.en: having great value, often because it is rare

ex.en: Gold and diamonds are precious materials.

precious.en: loved very much and treated as very important to you

ex.en: This old photo is very precious to me.
ex.ko: 이 오래된 사진은 나에게 매우 소중하다.

preference.en: a stronger liking for one thing over another

ex.en: What is your preference, tea or coffee?
ex.ko: 차와 커피 중 어느 쪽을 선호하나요?

prefix.en: a group of letters added to the beginning of a word that changes its meaning

ex.en: The prefix "un-" means "not", as in "unhappy".

pregnant.en: having a baby developing inside the body

ex.en: She is seven months pregnant.

prejudice.cue: 충분히 알기 전에 갖는 부정적 생각
prejudice.en: an unfair and unreasonable opinion about someone, often based on their race, religion, or gender

ex.en: The company was accused of racial prejudice in hiring.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 채용에서 인종 편견이 있었다는 비난을 받았다.

preparation.en: the process of getting something ready for use or making sure something will happen successfully

ex.en: We spent weeks in preparation for the wedding.
ex.ko: 우리는 결혼식 준비에 몇 주를 보냈다.

prepared.en: ready for something that is going to happen

ex.en: Make sure you are prepared for the exam tomorrow.
ex.ko: 내일 시험을 위해 준비를 확실히 해 두세요.

preposition.cue: 명사 앞에 놓여 관계를 나타내는 품사 (문법)
preposition.en: a word such as "in", "on", or "at" that shows the relationship between a noun and other words in a sentence

ex.en: "On" is a preposition in the sentence "The book is on the table."
ex.ko: 'The book is on the table.'라는 문장에서 'on'은 전치사이다.

preschool.en: a school for children who are not yet old enough for elementary school

ex.en: My daughter starts preschool next month.
ex.ko: 내 딸은 다음 달에 유치원에 들어간다.

preschool.en: relating to children who are not yet old enough for elementary school

ex.en: The center offers programs for preschool children.
ex.ko: 그 센터는 미취학 아동을 위한 프로그램을 제공한다.

prescription.en: a written instruction from a doctor for medicine that a patient needs

ex.en: The doctor gave me a prescription for antibiotics.
ex.ko: 의사는 나에게 항생제 처방전을 써 주었다.

presence.en: the fact of being in a particular place

ex.en: Your presence at the meeting is required.

presence.en: a strong and impressive quality that makes people notice someone

ex.en: The actor has a powerful presence on stage.
ex.ko: 그 배우는 무대에서 강한 존재감을 지니고 있다.

presentation.en: a talk in which something, such as information or a product, is shown or explained to a group of people

ex.en: She gave a presentation about her research.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 연구에 대해 발표를 했다.

preservation.en: the act of keeping something in its original state or protecting it from damage

ex.en: The organization works on the preservation of historic buildings.
ex.ko: 그 단체는 역사적 건물의 보존을 위해 일한다.

preserve.en: keep something in its original state or protect it from damage or destruction

ex.en: We must preserve the forest for future generations.
ex.ko: 우리는 미래 세대를 위해 숲을 보존해야 한다.

president.en: the head of state of a country, especially a republic

ex.en: The president gave a speech to the nation.

president.en: the person in charge of a company or organization

ex.en: She was recently promoted to president of the company.
ex.ko: 그녀는 최근 그 회사의 회장으로 승진했다.

ex.en: Press the button to start the machine.

press.en: newspapers, magazines, and the journalists who work for them

ex.en: The press reported on the scandal for weeks.
ex.ko: 언론은 몇 주 동안 그 스캔들을 보도했다.

previous.en: coming or happening before something else, or before the present time

ex.en: She lived in Seoul in her previous job.

priest.en: a person who has the authority to perform religious ceremonies, especially in the Christian church

ex.en: The priest blessed the newly married couple.

primarily.en: mostly, or more than anything else

ex.en: This course is aimed primarily at beginners.
ex.ko: 이 과정은 주로 초보자를 대상으로 한다.

primary school.en: a school for children, usually between the ages of five and eleven

ex.en: My son just started primary school this year.
ex.ko: 내 아들은 올해 막 초등학교에 입학했다.

primitive.en: very simple and not developed, relating to an early stage of human society or technology

ex.en: They used primitive tools made of stone.
ex.ko: 그들은 돌로 만든 원시적인 도구를 사용했다.

principle.en: a basic rule or belief that guides how you behave or think

ex.en: Honesty is one of her most important principles.
ex.ko: 정직함은 그녀에게 가장 중요한 원칙 중 하나이다.

prison.en: a building where criminals are kept as punishment

ex.en: He was sent to prison for ten years.

prisoner.en: a person who is kept in prison, or a person captured by an enemy

ex.en: The prisoner was released after ten years.

privacy.en: the state of being free from the attention or observation of other people

ex.en: She values her privacy and rarely posts online.
ex.ko: 그녀는 사생활을 소중히 여겨서 온라인에 거의 글을 올리지 않는다.

prize.en: something given to the winner of a competition or contest

ex.en: She won first prize in the writing contest.
She prizes her grandmother's ring above all her other jewelry.
ex.ko: 그녀는 다른 어떤 보석보다 할머니의 반지를 소중히 여긴다.

probability.en: how likely it is that something will happen

ex.en: There is a high probability of rain tomorrow.

proceed.en: continue with an action or process, especially after a pause

ex.en: Once everyone had arrived, the meeting proceeded.

process.en: treat raw materials or data in order to change or prepare them for a particular purpose

ex.en: The factory processes milk into cheese.

process.en: a series of actions or steps taken to achieve a particular result

ex.en: Learning a language is a long process.
The country proclaimed its independence in 1945.

procrastination.cue: 해야 할 일을 계속 미루는 것
procrastination.en: the habit of delaying something that needs to be done

ex.en: Procrastination is one of his worst habits.
ex.ko: 미루기는 그의 최악의 습관 중 하나이다.

producer.en: a person or company that makes or grows something

ex.en: This region is a major producer of coffee.

producer.en: a person who manages and finances the making of a film, show, or program

ex.en: She worked as a producer on several films.

productive.en: producing a lot, or achieving good results

ex.en: We had a very productive meeting this morning.
ex.ko: 우리는 오늘 아침 매우 생산적인 회의를 했다.

profession.en: a type of job that needs special training or education, such as law or medicine

ex.en: Teaching is a respected profession.

professor.en: a teacher of the highest rank at a college or university

ex.en: She is a professor of biology at the university.
The negotiations are progressing slowly.

progress.en: movement toward a goal, or improvement over time

ex.en: She has made great progress in her English.
ex.ko: 그녀는 영어 실력에서 큰 진전을 이루었다.

prohibition.en: an official rule or law that forbids something

ex.en: There is a prohibition on smoking in this building.
ex.ko: 이 건물에는 흡연 금지 규정이 있다.

ex.en: She is a prominent scientist in her field.

prominent.en: very noticeable and easy to see

ex.en: The building has a prominent position at the city center.
ex.ko: 그 건물은 도시 중심에서 눈에 띄는 위치에 있다.

promote.en: help something to develop, grow, or succeed

ex.en: The campaign aims to promote healthy eating.
ex.ko: 그 캠페인은 건강한 식습관을 촉진하는 것을 목표로 한다.

promote.en: give someone a higher position or rank at work

ex.en: She was promoted to manager last year.

promotion.en: a move to a higher position or rank at work

ex.en: He got a promotion after two years at the company.

promotion.en: activity that helps to make something known or sell more of it

ex.en: The store is running a special promotion this week.
ex.ko: 그 가게는 이번 주에 특별 판촉 행사를 하고 있다.

pronoun.en: a word such as "he", "she", or "it" that is used in place of a noun

ex.en: "They" is a pronoun used instead of a name.

proof.en: facts or information that show something is true

ex.en: She had no proof that he had lied.

properly.en: in a correct or suitable way

ex.en: Please make sure the door is properly closed.

property.en: land or buildings that someone owns

ex.en: This property has been in her family for generations.
ex.ko: 이 부동산은 그녀의 가문에서 여러 세대에 걸쳐 소유해 왔다.

property.en: a quality or characteristic that something has

ex.en: Water has the property of expanding when it freezes.
ex.ko: 물은 얼면 팽창하는 속성을 가지고 있다.

proportion.en: the amount or number of something compared to the total amount or number

ex.en: A large proportion of the population lives in cities.

proposal.en: a plan or idea that is suggested for people to consider

ex.en: The committee will review the proposal next week.
ex.ko: 위원회는 다음 주에 그 제안을 검토할 것이다.

proposal.en: an act of asking someone to marry you

ex.en: She said yes to his proposal.

propose.en: suggest a plan or idea for other people to consider

ex.en: He proposed a new plan to reduce costs.
ex.ko: 그는 비용을 줄이기 위한 새 계획을 제안했다.

ex.en: He proposed to her on the beach at sunset.
ex.ko: 그는 노을 지는 해변에서 그녀에게 청혼했다.

prosperity.en: the state of being successful, especially financially

ex.en: The country enjoyed decades of peace and prosperity.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 수십 년간 평화와 번영을 누렸다.

prosperous.en: successful, especially in a financial way

ex.en: The city became prosperous thanks to the new industry.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 새로운 산업 덕분에 번영하게 되었다.

protect.en: keep someone or something safe from harm or damage

ex.en: Sunscreen helps protect your skin from the sun.
ex.ko: 자외선 차단제는 피부를 태양으로부터 보호하는 데 도움을 준다.

protection.en: the act of keeping someone or something safe from harm

ex.en: Wearing a helmet gives you protection in an accident.
ex.ko: 헬멧을 쓰면 사고 시 보호를 받을 수 있다.

protective.en: intended to keep someone or something safe from harm

ex.en: Workers must wear protective gloves in the factory.
ex.ko: 근로자들은 공장에서 보호 장갑을 착용해야 한다.

protest.en: express strong disagreement or objection about something

ex.en: Residents protested against the new highway.

protest.cue: 여러 사람이 모여 반대를 표현하는 행사
protest.en: a public event or statement showing strong disagreement with something

ex.en: Thousands joined the protest in the city center.
ex.ko: 수천 명이 도심에서 열린 시위에 참여했다.
